summary: Typosquat of codebuff downloads and executes binary from unrelated GitHub account
details: codebuff-cli mirrors the README of the legitimate codebuff npm package (published by CodebuffAI) and provides bin entries `codebuff` and `codebuff-cli` pointing at cli/bin/codebuff.cjs. On first invocation, the wrapper downloads an opaque platform-specific executable from https://github.com/Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli/releases/download/v<version>/codebuff-<platform>-<arch> — a personal GitHub account unrelated to the legitimate publisher — into ~/.codebuff/bin/codebuff, chmod 0755, and execs it with the user's argv. No hash or signature verification is performed. The combination of name-impersonation (verbatim README copy of the legitimate codebuff package) plus fetch-and-exec of attacker-controlled binary from a publisher-mismatched location matches the typosquat-with-binary-dropper attack pattern. Installers running the CLI execute arbitrary native code controlled by a third party.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
